---
title: "Dota 2"
output: html_document
date: "2026-07-16"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# DOTA 2

## Packages

```{r}
library(glmnet)
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
library(rpart)
library(e1071)
library(ranger)
library(xgboost)
```

## EDA

```{r}
train <- read.csv("data/dota2Train.csv", header = FALSE)
test  <- read.csv("data/dota2Test.csv",  header = FALSE)

base_names <- c("winner", "cluster_id", "game_mode", "game_type")
hero_names <- paste0("hero_", seq_len(ncol(train) - 4))
colnames(train) <- c(base_names, hero_names)
colnames(test)  <- c(base_names, hero_names)
```

```{r}
heroes <- fromJSON("data/heroes.json")$heroes
heroes <- heroes[order(heroes$id), ]
```

```{r}
nrow(heroes)                      # number of heroes in the JSON
sum(grepl("^hero_", names(train)))  # number of hero columns in the data (should be 113)
head(heroes[, c("id", "localized_name")])
```

```{r}
dim(train)
dim(test)
```

```{r}
str(train[, 1:4])   # only leading columns; 113 hero cols omitted for readability
head(train[, 1:6])

```

```{r}
sum(is.na(train))
sum(is.na(test))
```

```{r}
winner_tbl <- table(train$winner)
winner_tbl
prop.table(winner_tbl)
```

```{r}
barplot(winner_tbl,
        col = "steelblue",
        xlab = "Winning team (1 vs -1)",
        ylab = "Games",
        main = "Match outcome distribution")
```

```{r}
sort(table(train$game_mode), decreasing = TRUE)
sort(table(train$game_type), decreasing = TRUE)
head(sort(table(train$cluster_id), decreasing = TRUE), 15)
```

```{r}
hero_cols <- train[, grep("^hero_", names(train))]

pick_freq <- data.frame(
  hero = names(hero_cols),
  picks = colSums(hero_cols != 0)
)
pick_freq <- pick_freq[order(-pick_freq$picks), ]
head(pick_freq, 15)
```

```{r}
top20 <- head(pick_freq, 20)
top20 <- top20[order(top20$picks), ]   # ascending so largest ends on top
barplot(top20$picks,
        names.arg = top20$hero,
        horiz = TRUE,
        las = 1,
        col = "darkorange",
        cex.names = 0.7,
        xlab = "Times picked",
        main = "Top 20 most-picked heroes")
```

```{r}
win_rate <- data.frame(hero = character(), games = integer(), win_rate = numeric())
for (h in names(hero_cols)) {
  picked   <- hero_cols[[h]] != 0
  side_won <- train$winner[picked] == hero_cols[[h]][picked]
  win_rate <- rbind(win_rate,
                    data.frame(hero = h,
                               games = sum(picked),
                               win_rate = mean(side_won)))
}
win_rate <- win_rate[win_rate$games > 0, ]
win_rate <- win_rate[order(-win_rate$win_rate), ]
head(win_rate, 15)
```

```{r}
picks_per_game <- rowSums(hero_cols != 0)
table(picks_per_game)
```

## Model Training

```{r}
prep <- function(df) {
  df$winner <- ifelse(df$winner == 1, 1, 0)
  df
}
train_m <- prep(train)
test_m  <- prep(test)

# Helper: misclassification error rate
err_rate <- function(pred_class, actual) mean(pred_class != actual)
```

### Logistic Regression

```{r}
# ---- Logistic regression (base R glm) ----
logit_fit <- glm(winner ~ ., data = train_m, family = binomial())

# Predicted probabilities -> class at 0.5 threshold
logit_train_p <- predict(logit_fit, train_m, type = "response")
logit_test_p  <- predict(logit_fit, test_m,  type = "response")
logit_train_c <- ifelse(logit_train_p > 0.5, 1, 0)
logit_test_c  <- ifelse(logit_test_p  > 0.5, 1, 0)

logit_train_err <- err_rate(logit_train_c, train_m$winner)
logit_test_err  <- err_rate(logit_test_c,  test_m$winner)

cat("Logistic  train error:", round(logit_train_err, 4),
    " test error:", round(logit_test_err, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Confusion matrix on test set
logit_cm <- table(Predicted = logit_test_c, Actual = test_m$winner)
print(logit_cm)
```

```{r}
# glmnet needs matrix X and numeric 0/1 y (reuse from xgboost prep)
# alpha = 1 is lasso (L1); cv.glmnet picks lambda by cross-validation
set.seed(1)
cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train, family = "binomial",
                    alpha = 1, type.measure = "class")
```

```{r}
# lambda.min = lowest CV error; lambda.1se = simplest model within 1 SE
plot(cv_fit)
cat("lambda.min:", cv_fit$lambda.min,
    " lambda.1se:", cv_fit$lambda.1se, "\n")
```

```{r}
# Test error at lambda.min
glmnet_test_p <- predict(cv_fit, X_test, s = "lambda.min", type = "response")
glmnet_test_c <- ifelse(glmnet_test_p > 0.5, 1, 0)
glmnet_test_err <- err_rate(glmnet_test_c, y_test)

cat("glmnet (lasso) test error:", round(glmnet_test_err, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Nonzero coefficients at lambda.1se = the heroes lasso kept
coefs <- coef(cv_fit, s = "lambda.1se")
kept <- data.frame(
  feature = rownames(coefs)[which(coefs != 0)],
  coefficient = coefs[which(coefs != 0)]
)
kept <- kept[order(-abs(kept$coefficient)), ]
cat("Features retained:", nrow(kept) - 1, "of", ncol(X_train), "\n")  # -1 for intercept
print(head(kept, 20))
```

```{r}
# Attach hero names to retained coefficients (heroes loaded + sorted by id earlier)
kept_heroes <- kept[grepl("^hero_", kept$feature), ]
kept_heroes$name <- heroes$localized_name[
  match(kept_heroes$feature, paste0("hero_", seq_len(nrow(heroes))))
]
print(head(kept_heroes[, c("name", "coefficient")], 20))
```

### Decision Tree

```{r}
# ---- Decision tree (rpart) ----
# winner must be a factor for classification (else rpart does regression)
train_t <- train_m; train_t$winner <- factor(train_t$winner)
test_t  <- test_m;  test_t$winner  <- factor(test_t$winner)

tree_fit <- rpart(winner ~ ., data = train_t, method = "class")

tree_train_c <- predict(tree_fit, train_t, type = "class")
tree_test_c  <- predict(tree_fit, test_t,  type = "class")

tree_train_err <- err_rate(tree_train_c, train_t$winner)
tree_test_err  <- err_rate(tree_test_c,  test_t$winner)

cat("Tree      train error:", round(tree_train_err, 4),
    " test error:", round(tree_test_err, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Visualize the tree (base rpart plotting, no extra packages)
plot(tree_fit, uniform = TRUE, margin = 0.1,
     main = "Decision tree: predicting winning team")
text(tree_fit, use.n = TRUE, cex = 0.7)
```

```{r}
# Complexity table — used for pruning (see tuning notes)
printcp(tree_fit)
plotcp(tree_fit)
```

### Naive Bayes

```{r}
# ---- Naive Bayes (e1071) ----
nb_fit <- naiveBayes(winner ~ ., data = train_t)

nb_train_c <- predict(nb_fit, train_t)
nb_test_c  <- predict(nb_fit, test_t)

nb_train_err <- err_rate(nb_train_c, train_t$winner)
nb_test_err  <- err_rate(nb_test_c,  test_t$winner)

cat("NaiveBayes train error:", round(nb_train_err, 4),
    " test error:", round(nb_test_err, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
{r}
# ---- Compare test error rates across models ----
errs <- c(Logistic = logit_test_err,
          Tree     = tree_test_err,
          NaiveBayes = nb_test_err)

bp <- barplot(errs, col = "steelblue", ylim = c(0, max(errs) * 1.2),
              ylab = "Test misclassification rate",
              main = "Model comparison (lower is better)")
text(bp, errs, labels = round(errs, 3), pos = 3)
abline(h = 0.5, lty = 2, col = "red")   # 0.5 = coin-flip baseline
```

### Random Forest

```{r}
library(ranger)

# probability = TRUE makes ranger output class probabilities,
# so the same 0.5 threshold rule applies as logistic regression
rf_fit <- ranger(
  winner ~ .,
  data = train_t,
  probability = TRUE,
  num.trees = 500,
  importance = "impurity"
)
```

```{r}
# Predict probabilities; column "1" is P(team 1 wins)
rf_train_p <- predict(rf_fit, train_t)$predictions[, "1"]
rf_test_p  <- predict(rf_fit, test_t)$predictions[, "1"]

rf_train_c <- ifelse(rf_train_p > 0.5, 1, 0)
rf_test_c  <- ifelse(rf_test_p  > 0.5, 1, 0)

# actuals back to 0/1 numeric to match predicted classes
rf_train_err <- err_rate(rf_train_c, as.numeric(as.character(train_t$winner)))
rf_test_err  <- err_rate(rf_test_c,  as.numeric(as.character(test_t$winner)))

cat("RandomForest train error:", round(rf_train_err, 4),
    " test error:", round(rf_test_err, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Confusion matrix on test set
rf_cm <- table(Predicted = rf_test_c,
               Actual = as.numeric(as.character(test_t$winner)))
print(rf_cm)
```

```{r}
# Top 20 heroes by impurity importance (feature signal, not direction)
imp <- sort(rf_fit$variable.importance, decreasing = TRUE)
imp20 <- rev(imp[1:20])   # ascending so largest ends on top

par(mar = c(4, 8, 3, 1))
barplot(imp20, horiz = TRUE, las = 1, col = "darkgreen",
        cex.names = 0.7, xlab = "Impurity importance",
        main = "Top 20 heroes by RF importance")
```

### XGBoost

```{r}
# xgboost needs numeric matrix X and numeric 0/1 label y
feat_cols <- setdiff(names(train_m), "winner")
X_train <- as.matrix(train_m[, feat_cols])
X_test  <- as.matrix(test_m[,  feat_cols])
y_train <- train_m$winner   # already 0/1 from prep()
y_test  <- test_m$winner

dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = X_train, label = y_train)
dtest  <- xgb.DMatrix(data = X_test,  label = y_test)
```

```{r}
# Baseline params — deliberately untuned, just defaults with logistic objective
xgb_fit <- xgboost(
  x = X_train,
  y = factor(y_train),   # new interface wants a factor for classification
  nrounds = 100,
  verbose = 0
)
```

```{r}
xgb_train_p <- predict(xgb_fit, X_train)
xgb_test_p  <- predict(xgb_fit, X_test)

xgb_train_c <- ifelse(xgb_train_p > 0.5, 1, 0)
xgb_test_c  <- ifelse(xgb_test_p  > 0.5, 1, 0)

xgb_train_err <- err_rate(xgb_train_c, y_train)
xgb_test_err  <- err_rate(xgb_test_c,  y_test)

cat("XGBoost train error:", round(xgb_train_err, 4),
    " test error:", round(xgb_test_err, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Compare all models on test error
all_errs <- c(Logistic   = logit_test_err,
              Tree       = tree_test_err,
              NaiveBayes = nb_test_err,
              RandomForest = rf_test_err,
              XGBoost    = xgb_test_err)

bp <- barplot(all_errs, col = "steelblue", ylim = c(0, max(all_errs) * 1.2),
              ylab = "Test misclassification rate", las = 2,
              main = "Model comparison (lower is better)")
text(bp, all_errs, labels = round(all_errs, 3), pos = 3)
abline(h = 0.5, lty = 2, col = "red")
```

### Pairwise Comparison

```{r}
# Hero matrix (games x 113), values 1 / -1 / 0
hero_idx <- grep("^hero_", names(train))
H <- as.matrix(train[, hero_idx])
hero_ids <- colnames(H)

# Presence masks and outcomes
T1 <- (H == 1)          # hero on team 1
T2 <- (H == -1)         # hero on team 2
win1 <- train$winner == 1   # team 1 won
win2 <- train$winner == -1  # team 2 won

n_games <- nrow(H)
```

```{r}
# Individual hero win rates (baseline for expected pair performance)
solo_games <- colSums(T1) + colSums(T2)
solo_wins  <- colSums(T1 & win1) + colSums(T2 & win2)
solo_wr    <- solo_wins / solo_games
```

```{r}
# ---- SYNERGY: two heroes on the SAME team ----
# Co-occurrence count on same team (symmetric matrix, diagonal = solo counts)
same_count <- t(T1) %*% T1 + t(T2) %*% T2
# Times that shared team won
same_wins  <- t(T1) %*% (T1 * win1) + t(T2) %*% (T2 * win2)

# Extract upper triangle (unique pairs, exclude diagonal)
ut <- which(upper.tri(same_count), arr.ind = TRUE)
syn <- data.frame(
  hero_a = hero_ids[ut[, 1]],
  hero_b = hero_ids[ut[, 2]],
  games  = same_count[ut],
  pair_wr = same_wins[ut] / same_count[ut]
)

# Expected win rate = average of the two solo rates
syn$expected_wr <- (solo_wr[ut[, 1]] + solo_wr[ut[, 2]]) / 2
syn$synergy <- syn$pair_wr - syn$expected_wr

# Filter to pairs with enough games to be meaningful
syn <- syn[syn$games >= 30, ]
```

```{r}
# Top synergies (overperform expectation) and anti-synergies (underperform)
syn <- syn[order(-syn$synergy), ]
cat("Strongest synergies:\n");     print(head(syn, 15), row.names = FALSE)
cat("\nStrongest anti-synergies:\n"); print(head(syn[order(syn$synergy), ], 15), row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# ---- COUNTERS: hero i on team 1 vs hero j on team 2 ----
opp_count <- t(T1) %*% T2                    # [i,j] = games i(team1) faced j(team2)
opp_wins  <- t(T1 * win1) %*% T2             # [i,j] = those games team 1 (hero i) won

# All i,j (not symmetric — direction matters), exclude same-hero diagonal
allij <- which(row(opp_count) != col(opp_count), arr.ind = TRUE)
ctr <- data.frame(
  hero    = hero_ids[allij[, 1]],
  against = hero_ids[allij[, 2]],
  games   = opp_count[allij],
  wr_vs   = opp_wins[allij] / opp_count[allij]
)
ctr <- ctr[ctr$games >= 30, ]
ctr <- ctr[order(-ctr$wr_vs), ]
cat("Strongest counters (hero beats 'against'):\n")
print(head(ctr, 15), row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Attach hero names (heroes loaded + sorted by id earlier)
id_to_name <- setNames(heroes$localized_name,
                       paste0("hero_", seq_len(nrow(heroes))))
syn$name_a <- id_to_name[syn$hero_a]
syn$name_b <- id_to_name[syn$hero_b]
ctr$name    <- id_to_name[ctr$hero]
ctr$vs_name <- id_to_name[ctr$against]

cat("Top synergies (named):\n")
print(head(syn[, c("name_a", "name_b", "games", "synergy")], 15), row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Distribution of synergy values — is there real structure or is it ~0?
hist(syn$synergy, breaks = 50, col = "steelblue",
     xlab = "Pair win rate − expected", main = "Synergy distribution")
abline(v = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2)
cat("SD of synergy:", round(sd(syn$synergy), 4),
    " | mean abs:", round(mean(abs(syn$synergy)), 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
hist(solo_wr, breaks = 30, col = "steelblue",
     xlab = "Individual hero win rate", main = "Distribution of solo win rates")
abline(v = 0.5, col = "red", lwd = 2)
cat("Solo win rate range:", round(range(solo_wr), 3),
    " SD:", round(sd(solo_wr), 3), "\n")
```
